title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government is providing a response to a report on Artificial Intelligence (AI) and its effects on public standards. The response outlines how the government will handle AI's implications for society based on the recommendations in the report.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It examines the implications for copyright law and its adaptation to the evolving field of AI.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education has a policy surrounding the use of generative AI in the education sector, outlining its stance and guidelines. The policy is likely aimed at balancing the potential benefits of generative AI with concerns about its impact on learning and academic integrity.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines a government plan for using artificial intelligence (AI) in transportation and identifying potential risks. It aims to collaborate with the transportation sector to maximize AI benefits while addressing its drawbacks.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This document is a progress report on the use of copyrighted works in creating artificial intelligence, in accordance with a specific section of a law related to data use. It explores the intersection of copyright and AI development under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government has implemented a new policy regulating the use of artificial intelligence in medical devices, following recommendations from the Regulatory Horizons Council. This policy aims to ensure the safe and effective use of AI in healthcare.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This policy document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence (AI) in exams and qualifications. It is a response to a request from government officials to provide guidance on AI use in the qualifications sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to the House of Lords Artificial Intelligence Select Committee's report, addressing concerns and recommendations regarding AI in the UK. The response outlines the government's plans to tackle the challenges and opportunities presented by AI, following the committee's call for no complacency.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a review into how artificial intelligence fits with existing public standards. The review aims to provide guidance on AI and its implications for the community.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The UK's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has shared its views on how to regulate Artificial Intelligence. They contributed to a government-led inquiry into governing AI to create a safer and more responsible tech future.
